1. Skill Coverage Comprehensiveness (30.1a_covered: SQL_basics, SQL_advanced, Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, Seaborn, statistics, machine_learning
1. Skill Coverage Comprehensiveness (30.1b_comment: Overall coverage is directionally correct (SQL -> Pandas/NumPy -> viz/stats -> ML + projects), but the provided plan content is largely a template with only Day 1-2 detailed. SQL shows basics and joins, but there’s no explicit day-by-day inclusion of GROUP BY/aggregations, subqueries/CTEs, or window functions—so depth is incomplete. Python data processing is stated as a Week 2 objective, but no explicit daily tasks confirming core Pandas skills (I/O, missing values, groupby, merge/join, reshape, datetime, text) or NumPy essentials beyond general wording. Visualization and statistics are mentioned as Week 3 goals, but there’s no concrete coverage proof for probability distributions and hypothesis testing (e.g., t-test, chi-square, A/B testing workflow) alongside plotting practice. ML is only described as an overview + sklearn intro with a project; it does not explicitly name/teach at least 2-3 algorithms (e.g., linear/logistic regression + tree), so it gets minimal credit.

2. Difficulty Progression Reasonableness.2a_structure_detection: 2/5 — has complete week division (9 weeks), detected 7 distinct Day(s)
2. Difficulty Progression Reasonableness.2b_comment: Starting point is mostly appropriate: Week 1 begins with SQL SELECT/WHERE/ORDER BY and quickly moves to JOINs, which fits an intermediate Python learner pivoting to analytics (minor deduction because Day 1 is very beginner-oriented and the plan doesn’t mention a SQL environment/setup). Weekly progression matches the recommended route (SQL -> Pandas/NumPy -> Viz/Stats -> ML -> projects) and is coherent; small deduction because Week 4 mixes ML intro and projects without showing a clear ramp (e.g., feature engineering/evaluation before project modeling). Intra-week/day progression cannot be fully verified because most days are elided (only Day 1-2 are detailed, others are placeholders), so evidence of a smooth daily scaffold is insufficient. Comprehensive project is present (end-to-end Kaggle-style projects on Day 28-30) and integrates skills; minor deduction because the e-commerce project includes "purchase behavior prediction" which may be too ambitious without explicitly scheduled model evaluation/validation steps earlier.

3. Resource Authenticity & Quality (20 p.3b_comment: Authenticity (4/4): All explicitly linked resources are real and well-known (freeCodeCamp YouTube SQL tutorial, W3Schools SQL pages, SQLBolt, Mode SQL tutorial). Named platforms in the appendix (Coursera, DataCamp, Kaggle Learn, LeetCode, HackerRank) are also legitimate, though not all are linked. Specificity (3/4): Day 1 is quite specific (exact video + 'first 1 hour', exact W3Schools page, SQLBolt lessons 1–3). Day 2 is mostly specific (SQLBolt lessons 6–7; Mode 'SQL Joins' page) but the 'Programming with Mosh (15 min)' item lacks a direct link and exact video identifier, and many later days are left as '...'(no concrete resources), which limits plan-wide specificity. Diversity (4/4): Strong mix of video (YouTube), reading/reference (W3Schools, Mode, books), interactive practice (SQLBolt, LeetCode/HackerRank), and project/data sources (Kaggle, Yahoo Finance). Overall high quality, with the main deduction coming from missing links/IDs and incomplete daily resource specification beyond the first couple of days.

4. Practicality & Executability (15 pts).4b_comment:
Daily practice tasks (1/4): Only Day 1 and Day 2 explicitly list concrete exercises with clear deliverables (e.g., SQLBolt lessons, specific join task). Most other days are placeholders (“Day 3-7 continued…”, “Day 8…”, “Day 15…”, “Day 22…”) with no specified practice tasks, so the plan as provided does not ensure daily hands-on work and is not executable day-by-day.

Time arrangement feasibility (1/3): Where shown, estimates (2.5h) fit a 2–3h/day budget. However, the majority of days have no time estimates or task granularity, so workload balance cannot be verified; the last-week projects are likely to exceed 2–3h/day unless tightly scoped with explicit milestones.

Comprehensive project quality (2/3): Uses real-data sources (Kaggle, Yahoo Finance) and intends skill integration. But integration of SQL is not operationalized (many Kaggle datasets are CSV-first; you need an explicit step like loading into SQLite/Postgres and writing required queries), and deliverables/rubric (EDA questions, required charts, model baseline, report format) are not defined, reducing executability.

5. Document Format & Extras (10 pts).5b_structure_completeness: 4/4 — User profile:Y 4-week division:Y(9 weeks) Appendix/Tips:Y Daily time:Y
